title: Gentle Herbal British Pickle Casserole
description: A dense and light British dish featuring cream of tartar and pickle, perfect for a keto-friendly meal. This casserole combines herbal flavors with a tangy twist.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 350 degrees Fahrenheit.
Brown the ground beef in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the pickle relish, cream of tartar, dried thyme, dried rosemary, onion powder, and garlic powder to the skillet and stir to combine.
Transfer the mixture to a large casserole dish.
Top the mixture with shredded cheddar cheese.
In a separate bowl, whisk together the sour cream and eggs.
Pour the sour cream and egg mixture over the cheese.
Season with salt and black pepper to taste.
Bake the casserole in the preheated oven for 30 minutes.
Remove the casserole from the oven and let it cool for a few minutes before serving.
Cook ground beef to an internal temperature of 71°C / 160°F (no longer pink in the middle) before serving.
Note: this recipe is for refrigerator pickling/preserving only — store in the refrigerator and consume within 1 week. Do not water-bath can without an acid (vinegar or lemon juice).
